description:
In SocketManager.unregister, we use connection.copy_with(unregistered: true) to create a replacement for the connection in the array. However, copy_with on a record creates a shallow copy - the websocket reference is shared. More importantly, the writer_fiber reads connection.unregistered on the ORIGINAL connection object, but we've modified what the array points to.

The issue: we remove the original connection from @connections and add the modified copy. But writer_fiber holds a reference to the original connection object. So when writer_fiber checks connection.unregistered, it sees false (original value), not true (modified copy value).

Location: src/websocket/socket_manager.cr:unregister
title: Bug: Connection unregistered flag not thread-safe in copy_with
